{"query":"Tesla FSD monthly subscription price","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1075,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.motortrend.com/news/tesla-ceo-elon-musk-says-fsd-subscription-prices-will-rise-capabilities-improve","title":"Tesla CEO Elon Musk Says FSD Subscription Prices Will Rise “as Capabilities Improve”","content":"After February 14, the only way to get Tesla’s automated driving software will be a monthly subscription that will start at $99."},{"url":"https://www.lowermysubs.com/blog/tesla-fsd-worth-it-2026","title":"$99/Month Tesla FSD: Worth It in 2026 or Skip It? | LowerMySubs Blog","content":"Jul 2, 2026 — Tesla FSD is subscription only in 2026: $99/month, or $49 for EAP owners. At $99 per month ($49 if you already own Enhanced Autopilot), a full ...
The one-time purchase ended on February 14, 2026 at a final price of $8,000, and Tesla now sells FSD strictly as a subscription.
Enhanced Autopilot owner rate: $49/month.
One more 2026 change matters for new buyers: Tesla removed Basic Autopilot (including Autosteer lane centering) from new Model 3 and Model Y orders in North America in late January 2026.
Basic Autopilot historically bundled Traffic-Aware Cruise Control and Autosteer for free, but new Model 3 and Model Y orders in North America now include cruise control only.
At $99 per month, three full years of FSD costs $3,564, still $4,436 less than the final $8,000 purchase price.
Ownership length — Subscription at $99/mo — Subscription at $49/mo (EAP owners) — One-time purchase (ended Feb 14, 2026)
AI4 (HW4) cars run full FSD v14.
If you are buying a used Tesla, verify FSD status in the car's software screen before paying any premium, because listings are frequently wrong about what transfers."},{"url":"https://www.basenor.com/blogs/news/tesla-fsd-affordability-in-2026-5-details-that-matter","title":"Tesla FSD Affordability in 2026: 5 Details That Matter","content":"4 days ago — Tesla is offering FSD at a reduced rate of $49 per month — effective since January 29, 2026. the $99 subscription price is not permanent.
The shift from an $8,000 one-time purchase to a $99 monthly subscription has quietly changed the math for millions of potential FSD adopters.
The current U.S. subscription rate for Full Self-Driving (Supervised) is $99/month.
This report was curated by the BASENOR Editorial Desk from the sources listed above."},{"url":"https://www.teslaacessories.com/blogs/news/tesla-fsd-in-2026-approvals-subscriptions-and-what-it-means-for-us-and-european-owners-1","title":"Should You Buy FSD in 2026? A Practical Guide for US and European Tesla Owners – TESMAG","content":"Tesla is ending the option to buy FSD as a one‑time “lifetime of the vehicle” purchase after February 14, 2026, and is moving to a subscription‑only model at 99 USD per month in the US.
At the same time, Tesla has removed the traditional Autopilot package from new vehicles in the US and Canada and now includes Traffic‑Aware Cruise Control (TACC) as the standard baseline, with FSD as the main paid upgrade.
As of January 2026, Tesla’s North American support page lists FSD (Supervised) at 99 USD per month, with eligibility limited to vehicles that have the necessary FSD computer hardware.
Of course, this comparison assumes the price stays at 99 USD per month, but Elon Musk has explicitly said the subscription fee will rise as FSD’s capabilities improve.
More than 6–7 years: consider the one‑time 8,000 USD purchase if you will use FSD heavily."},{"url":"https://www.teslaoracle.com/2021/07/19/tesla-opens-the-monthly-subscription-option-for-full-self-driving/","title":"Tesla opens the monthly subscription option for Full Self-Driving - Tesla Oracle","content":"If your vehicle has Full Self-Driving computer 3.0 or above, plus Basic Autopilot or Enhanced Autopilot, you are eligible to subscribe to FSD capability.
Your vehicle’s current Autopilot package of Basic Autopilot or Enhanced Autopilot will determine the FSD capability subscription price.
Basic Autopilot to FSD capability $199.00 per month
Many Tesla owners (mostly Model S and X) purchased the Enhanced Autopilot for $5,000 and later on Tesla reduced the price to $3,000.
Tesla has listed detailed FAQs on its support website, a must read before opting for the monthly Full Self-Driving option.
Tesla (TSLA) has added Miami, Florida, as one more..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Tesla FSD monthly subscription price","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.tesla.com/support/full-self-driving-subscriptions","title":"Full Self-Driving (Supervised) Subscriptions","content":"FSD (Supervised) is available as a monthly subscription of $99. You can subscribe to FSD (Supervised) from the Tesla app or your vehicle's touchscreen.","score":0.9365236,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.notateslaapp.com/news/3539/tesla-reduces-fsd-subscription-to-49month-for-vehicles-with-enhanced-autopilot","title":"Tesla Reduces FSD Subscription to $49/Month for Vehicles With Enhanced Autopilot - Not a Tesla App","content":"# Tesla Reduces FSD Subscription to $49/Month for Vehicles With Enhanced Autopilot. In a quiet update to the Tesla mobile app this weekend, the company has officially lowered the subscription pricing of FSD (Supervised) to $49 per month for owners who have already purchased Enhanced Autopilot (EAP) (differences between Basic Autopilot, EAP, and FSD). This is a 50% discount off the standard $99/month subscription rate for all other owners. This new pricing is strictly for owners who already paid for the Enhanced Autopilot package - a middle-tier option that Tesla sold for years (typically for $6,000 USD), before discontinuing it in recent years. Owners of EAP have the option to upgrade to FSD for only $2,000 USD. For many EAP owners, it makes sense to purchase the upgrade soon, as Tesla plans to permanently remove the option to purchase FSD outright on February 14, 2026. Contact Us Newsletter RSS Feed Advertise Privacy Policy.","score":0.92825955,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.motortrend.com/news/tesla-ceo-elon-musk-says-fsd-subscription-prices-will-rise-capabilities-improve","title":"Tesla CEO Elon Musk Says FSD Subscription Prices Will Rise “as Capabilities Improve”","content":"# Tesla CEO Elon Musk Says FSD Subscription Prices Will Rise “as Capabilities Improve”. Less than 10 days after saying owners won’t be able to outright buy FSD and new owners will need to subscribe to it, Tesla CEO Elon Musk has admitted the $99 per month price tag will not remain static. While he didn’t specify how much the price will rise or how often, Musk still claims there will be “a massive value jump” when new features are introduced. After February 14, the only way to get Tesla’s automated driving software will be a monthly subscription that will start at $99. While Musk didn’t say the monthly fee will increase, a response to Sawyer Merritt on Twitter confirms the cost of FSD’s monthly subscription will rise “as FSD’s capabilities improve.”. Musk didn’t clarify in the post just how often those capabilities will “improve,” what counts as an improvement worth increasing the price for, and how much those improvements will affect the price.","score":0.91901255,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reddit.com/r/TeslaLounge/comments/1r2w51h/help_me_understand_why_new_buyers_pay_for_fsd","title":"Help Me Understand Why New Buyers Pay For FSD","content":"Tesla officially introduced the Full Self-Driving (FSD) subscription service at a price of $199 per month in the U.S.. $199/month, or $99/month","score":0.90771407,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://teslahubs.com/blogs/tips/tesla-reduces-fsd-subscription-to-49-month-for-vehicles-with-enhanced-autopilot","title":"Tesla Reduces FSD Subscription to $49/Month for Vehicles ...","content":"Tesla Reduces FSD Subscription to $49/Month for Vehicles.
